services.name: pub.art:listRegisteredAdapters
services.description: WmART. Returns the display name and adapter type name of all registered adapters.

output_parameters.name: registeredAdapterList
output_parameters.description: Document List Information for each adapter registered with the WmART package.

output_parameters.name: adapterDisplayName
output_parameters.description: String The localized name that the Integration Server Administrator displays.

output_parameters.name: adapterTypeName
output_parameters.description: String The name of the adapter as registered with the WmART package. This value can be used as input for the inventory services that take adapterTypeName as input.

services.name: pub.art.connection:disableConnection
services.description: WmART. Disables a connection node.

input_parameters.name: connectionAlias
input_parameters.description: String Name of the connection node you want to disable.

services.name: pub.art.connection:enableConnection
services.description: WmART. Enables an existing connection node.

input_parameters.name: connectionAlias
input_parameters.description: String Name of the connection node you want to enable.

services.name: pub.art.connection:getConnectionStatistics
services.description: WmART. Returns current usage statistics for a connection node.

input_parameters.name: aliasName
input_parameters.description: String Name of the connection node for which you want usage statistics returned.

output_parameters.name: connectionStatistics
output_parameters.description: Document List Information for each connection node.

output_parameters.name: TotalConnections
output_parameters.description: Integer Current number of connection instances.

output_parameters.name: BusyConnections
output_parameters.description: Integer Number of connections currently in use by services, notifications, and listeners.

output_parameters.name: FreeConnections
output_parameters.description: Integer Total number of connections created and available for use.

output_parameters.name: TotalHits
output_parameters.description: Integer Number of times this connection node successfully provided connections since the last reset.

output_parameters.name: TotalMisses
output_parameters.description: Integer Number of times this connection node unsuccessfully provided connections since the last reset (when the request timed out).

services.name: pub.art.connection:listAdapterConnections
services.description: WmART. Lists connection nodes associated with a specified adapter.

input_parameters.name: adapterTypeName
input_parameters.description: String The name of the adapter as registered with the WmART package.

output_parameters.name: connectionDataList
output_parameters.description: Document List Information for each connection node registered with the specified adapter.

output_parameters.name: connectionAlias
output_parameters.description: String The name of the connection node.

output_parameters.name: packageName
output_parameters.description: String The name of the package in which the connection node resides.

output_parameters.name: connectionState
output_parameters.description: String Current state of the connection node. The state will have one of these values: disabled - Connection node is disabled enabled - Connection node is enabled. shuttingdown - Connection node is in the process of shutting down. unknown - Connection node is registered but has not yet established its state.

services.name: pub.art.connection:queryConnectionState
services.description: WmART. Returns the current connection state (enabled/disabled) and error status for a connection node.

input_parameters.name: connectionAlias
input_parameters.description: String Name of the connection node for which you want the connection state and error status returned.

output_parameters.name: connectionState
output_parameters.description: String Current connection state (enabled/disabled).

output_parameters.name: hasError
output_parameters.description: Boolean Flag indicating if any error was detected on connection. The values are: true if an error was detected. false if no error was detected.

services.name: pub.art.listener:disableListener
services.description: WmART. Disables a listener.

input_parameters.name: listenerName
input_parameters.description: String Name of the listener you want to disable. The listener should have a state of enabled or suspended.

input_parameters.name: forceDisable
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Flag to disable the listener regardless of whether it is still waiting for data from a back-end resource. The string may have one of these values: true to disable the listener. false to keep the listener enabled.

services.name: pub.art.listener:enableListener
services.description: WmART. Enables an existing listener.

input_parameters.name: listenerName
input_parameters.description: String Name of the listener you want to enable.

usage_notes: Usage Notes If you do not enable the connection resource associated with the listener, this service will return without performing any action, and the listener will remain disabled. Therefore, you should invoke pub.art.listener:queryListenerState before calling this service to confirm that the listener has been enabled.

services.name: pub.art.listener:listAdapterListeners
services.description: WmART. Lists listeners associated with a specified adapter.

input_parameters.name: adapterTypeName
input_parameters.description: String The name of the adapter as registered with the WmART package.

output_parameters.name: listenerDataList
output_parameters.description: Document List Information for each listener registered with the specified adapter.

output_parameters.name: listenerNodeName
output_parameters.description: String The name of the listener.

output_parameters.name: packageName
output_parameters.description: String The name of the package in which the listener resides.

output_parameters.name: listenerEnabled
output_parameters.description: String Current state of the listener. The state will have one of these values: disabled if the listener is disabled. enabled if the listener is enabled. enablePending if the listener is in the process of starting. disablePending if the listener is in the process of disabling. suspended if the listener is suspended. suspendPending if the listener is in the process of suspending.

services.name: pub.art.listener:queryListenerState
services.description: WmART. Returns the current state for a listener.

input_parameters.name: listenerName
input_parameters.description: String Name of the listener for which you want the current state returned.

output_parameters.name: listenerState
output_parameters.description: String Current state of the listener. The state will have one of these values: disabled if the listener is disabled. enabled if the listener is enabled. enablePending if the listener is in the process of starting. disablePending if the listener is in the process of disabling. suspended if the listener is suspended. suspendPending if the listener is in the process of suspending.

services.name: pub.art.listener:resumeListener
services.description: WmART. Resumes a specified listener.

input_parameters.name: listenerName
input_parameters.description: String The name of the suspended listener you want to resume. The service returns an error if you specify an invalid listener.

usage_notes: Usage Notes If the requested transition is not valid (for example, trying to resume a disabled listener or a listener that is already resumed), the service ignores the request. After you use this service, you can use pub.art.listener:queryListenerState to verify pub.art.listener:resumeListener correctly changed the state of the listener.

services.name: pub.art.listener:setListenerNodeConnection
services.description: WmART. Changes the connection node used by a specified listener.

input_parameters.name: listenerName
input_parameters.description: String Name of the listener for which you want to change the connection node.

input_parameters.name: connectionAlias
input_parameters.description: String Name of the new connection node to use with the listener.

usage_notes: Usage Notes Calling this service for a listener that is disabled is permitted. Calling this service for a listener that is suspended changes the state of the listener to disabled. The user must enable the listener before using it.

services.name: pub.art.listener:suspendListener
services.description: WmART. Suspends a specified listener.

input_parameters.name: listenerName
input_parameters.description: String The name of the listener you want to suspend. The service returns an error if you specify an invalid listener.

usage_notes: Usage Notes If the requested transition is not valid (for example, trying to suspend a disabled listener or a listener that is already suspended), the service ignores the request. After you use this service, you can use pub.art.listener:queryListenerState to verify pub.art.listener:suspendListener correctly changed the state of the listener.

services.name: pub.art.notification:disableListenerNotification
services.description: WmART. Disables a listener notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
input_parameters.description: String The name of the listener notification you want to disable.

services.name: pub.art.notification:disablePollingNotification
services.description: WmART. Disables a polling notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
input_parameters.description: String The name of the polling notification you want to disable. The polling notification should have a state of enabled or suspended.

services.name: pub.art.notification:disablePublishEvents
services.description: WmART. Disables the publishing of events for an adapter notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
input_parameters.description: String The name of the notification for which you want to disable the publishing of events.

services.name: pub.art.notification:enableListenerNotification
services.description: WmART. Enables an existing listener notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
input_parameters.description: String The name of the listener notification you want to enable.

services.name: pub.art.notification:enablePollingNotification
services.description: WmART. Enables an existing polling notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
input_parameters.description: String Name of the polling notification you want to enable.

usage_notes: Usage Notes You must schedule the polling notification before you can run this service. See your adapter user documentation for instructions to schedule the polling notification.

services.name: pub.art.notification:enablePublishEvents
services.description: WmART. Enables the publishing of events for an adapter notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
input_parameters.description: String The name of the notification for which you want to enable the publishing of events.

services.name: pub.art.notification:listAdapterListenerNotifications
services.description: WmART. Lists the listener notifications associated with a specified adapter.

input_parameters.name: adapterTypeName
input_parameters.description: String The name of the adapter as registered with the WmART package.

output_parameters.name: notificationDataList
output_parameters.description: Document List Information for each listener notification registered with the specified adapter.

output_parameters.name: notificationNodeName
output_parameters.description: String The name of the listener notification.

output_parameters.name: packageName
output_parameters.description: String The name of the package in which the listener notification resides.

output_parameters.name: notificationEnabled
output_parameters.description: String The current state of the listener notification. The state will have one of these values: no if the listener notification is disabled. yes if the listener notification is enabled.

services.name: pub.art.notification:listAdapterPollingNotifications
services.description: WmART. Lists the polling notifications associated with a specified adapter.

input_parameters.name: adapterTypeName
input_parameters.description: String The name of the adapter as registered with the WmART package.

output_parameters.name: notificationDataList
output_parameters.description: Document List Information for each polling notification registered with the specified adapter.

output_parameters.name: notificationNodeName
output_parameters.description: String The name of the polling notification.

output_parameters.name: packageName
output_parameters.description: String The name of the package in which the polling notification resides.

output_parameters.name: notificationEnabled
output_parameters.description: String The current state of the polling notification. The state will have one of these values: no if the polling notification is disabled. yes if the polling notification is enabled. pending if the polling notification is in the process of shutting down. suspended if the polling notification is suspended.

services.name: pub.art.notification:queryListenerNotificationState
services.description: WmART. Returns the current state (enabled/disabled) for a listener notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
input_parameters.description: String The name of the listener notification for which you want the current state (enabled/disabled) returned.

output_parameters.name: notificationState
output_parameters.description: String The current state (enabled/disabled) for the listener notification.

services.name: pub.art.notification:queryPollingNotificationState
services.description: WmART. Returns the current state for a polling notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
input_parameters.description: String The name of the polling notification for which you want the current state and schedule settings returned.

output_parameters.name: notificationState
output_parameters.description: String The current state (enabled, disabled, pending disable, pending suspend, or suspended) for the polling notification.

output_parameters.name: scheduleSettings
output_parameters.description: IData Object that contains the notification's schedule settings as follows:

output_parameters.name: notificationInterval
output_parameters.description: Integer Polling frequency of the notification.

output_parameters.name: notificationOverlap
output_parameters.description: Boolean Flags whether the notification can overlap. The values are: true if the notification can overlap. false if the notification cannot overlap.

output_parameters.name: notificationImmediate
output_parameters.description: Boolean Flags whether the notification can fire immediately. The values are: true if the notification can fire immediately. false if the notification cannot fire immediately.

services.name: pub.art.notification:resumePollingNotification
services.description: WmART. Resumes a specified polling notification node.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
input_parameters.description: String The name of the polling notification you want to resume. The service returns an error if you specify an invalid polling notification.

usage_notes: Usage Notes If the requested transition is not valid (for example, trying to resume a disabled polling notification or a polling notification that is already resumed), the service ignores the request. After you use this service, you can use pub.art.notification:queryPollingNotificationState to verify pub.art.notification:resumePollingNotification correctly changed the state of the polling notification to enabled.

services.name: pub.art.notification:setListenerNotificationNodeListener
services.description: WmART. Changes the listener used by a specified listener notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
input_parameters.description: String Name of the listener notification for which you want to change the listener.

input_parameters.name: listenerNode
input_parameters.description: String Name of the new listener to use with the listener notification.

usage_notes: Usage Notes This service returns an error if the listener notification is enabled. You can use this service for synchronous and asynchronous listener notifications.

services.name: pub.art.notification:setPollingNotificationNodeConnection
services.description: WmART. Changes the connection node used by a specified polling notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
input_parameters.description: String Name of the polling notification for which you want to change the connection node.

input_parameters.name: connectionAlias
input_parameters.description: String Name of the new connection node to use with the polling notification.

usage_notes: Usage Notes The polling notification must be in a disabled or suspended state before you call this service. This service returns an error if the polling notification is enabled. If you use this service on a suspended polling notification, the service changes the state of the polling notification to disabled.

services.name: pub.art.notification:suspendPollingNotification
services.description: WmART. Suspends a specified polling notification.

input_parameters.name: notificationName
input_parameters.description: String The name of the polling notification you want to suspend. The service returns an error if you specify an invalid polling notification.

usage_notes: Usage Notes If the requested transition is not valid (for example, trying to suspend a disabled polling notification or a polling notification that is already suspended), the service ignores the request. After you use this service, you can use pub.art.notification:queryPollingNotificationState to verify pub.art.notification:suspendPollingNotification correctly changed the state of the polling notification to suspended.

services.name: pub.art.service:listAdapterServices
services.description: WmART. Lists adapter services associated with a specified adapter.

input_parameters.name: adapterTypeName
input_parameters.description: String The name of the adapter as registered with the WmART package.

output_parameters.name: serviceDataList
output_parameters.description: Document List Information for each adapter service registered with the specified adapter.

output_parameters.name: serviceNodeName
output_parameters.description: String The name of the adapter service.

output_parameters.name: packageName
output_parameters.description: String The name of the package in which the adapter service resides.

services.name: pub.art.service:setAdapterServiceNodeConnection
services.description: WmART. Changes the connection node used by a specified adapter service.

input_parameters.name: serviceName
input_parameters.description: String Name of an existing adapter service for which you want to change the connection node.

input_parameters.name: connectionAlias
input_parameters.description: String Name of the new connection node to use with the adapter service.

usage_notes: Usage Notes The new connection node must be enabled before you call this service.

services.name: pub.art.transaction:commitTransaction
services.description: WmART. Commits an explicit transaction.

input_parameters.name: commitTransactionInput
input_parameters.description: Document List Information for each commit request.

input_parameters.name: transactionName
input_parameters.description: String The name of an explicit transaction that you want to commit. The transactionName must have been previously used in a call to pub.art.transaction:startTransaction. This value must be mapped from the most recent pub.art.transaction:startTransaction that has not previously been committed or rolled back.

usage_notes: Usage Notes This service is available only if your adapter supports built-in transaction management services, which you can confirm by checking the user guide for the adapter. This service must be used in conjunction with the pub.art.transaction:startTransaction service. If the transactionName parameter was not provided in a prior call to pub.art.transaction:startTransaction, a run-time error will be returned.

services.name: pub.art.transaction:rollbackTransaction
services.description: WmART. Rolls back an explicit transaction.

input_parameters.name: rollbackTransactionInput
input_parameters.description: Document List Information for each rollback request.

input_parameters.name: transactionName
input_parameters.description: String The name of an explicit transaction that you want to roll back. The transactionName must have been previously used in a call to pub.art.transaction:startTransaction. This value must be mapped from the most recent pub.art.transaction:startTransaction that has not previously been committed or rolled back.

usage_notes: Usage Notes This service is available only if your adapter supports built-in transaction management services, which you can confirm by checking the adapter's user guide. This service must be used in conjunction with the pub.art.transaction:startTransaction service. If the given transactionName parameter was not provided in a prior call to pub.art.transaction:startTransaction, a run-time error will be returned.

services.name: pub.art.transaction:setTransactionTimeout
services.description: WmART. Manually sets a transaction timeout interval for implicit and explicit transactions.

input_parameters.name: timeoutSeconds
input_parameters.description: Integer The number of seconds that the implicit or explicit transaction stays open before the transaction manager marks it for rollback.

usage_notes: Usage Notes This service is available only if your adapter supports built-in transaction management services, which you can confirm by checking the user guide for the adapter. When you use this service, you are temporarily overriding the Integration Server transaction timeout interval. You must call this service within a flow before the start of any implicit or explicit transactions. Implicit transactions start when you call an adapter service in a flow. Explicit transactions start when you call the pub.art.transaction:startTransaction service. If the execution of a transaction takes longer than the transaction timeout interval, all transacted operations are rolled back. This service only overrides the transaction timeout interval for the flow service in which you call it.

services.name: pub.art.transaction:startTransaction
services.description: WmART. Starts an explicit transaction.

input_parameters.name: startTransactionInput
input_parameters.description: Document List Information for each start transaction request.

input_parameters.name: transactionName
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Specifies the name of the transaction to be started. If you leave this parameter blank, Integration Server will generate a name for you. In most implementations it is not necessary to provide your own transaction name.

output_parameters.name: startTransactionOutput
output_parameters.description: Document List Information for each start transaction request.

output_parameters.name: transactionName
output_parameters.description: String The name of the transaction the service just started.

usage_notes: Usage Notes This service is available only if your adapter supports built-in transaction management services, which you can confirm by checking the user guide for the adapter. This service is intended for use with the pub.art.transaction:commitTransaction or pub.art.transaction:rollbackTransaction service. The transactionName value returned by a call to this service can be provided to pub.art.transaction:commitTransaction (to commit the transaction) or pub.art.transaction:rollbackTransaction (to roll back the transaction).
